item.title: 英国军方称，三艘油轮在霍尔木兹海峡最近的袭击中被击中
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The U.S. has revoked a license that had authorized the sale of Iranian oil, the Treasury Department said Tuesday.
The Treasury issued the 60-day license last month, waiving sanctions on Iranian oil as part of an interim agreement to end the fighting between the two countries. Treasury officials did not immediately respond to an Associated Press inquiry on why the license was revoked.
The revocation came hours after the British military said three tankers were struck by projectiles in theStrait of Hormuz. They were the latest attacks targeting vessels moving through the fuel-shipping waterway that is central to negotiations seeking a permanent end to thewar between the U.S. and Iran.
THIS IS A BREAKING NEWS UPDATE. AP’s earlier story follows below.
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Three tankers were struck by projectiles Tuesday in theStrait of Hormuz, the British military said, in the latest attacks targeting vessels moving through the fuel-shipping waterway that is central to negotiations seeking a permanent end to thewar between the U.S. and Iran.
The new assaults were the most in a single day since late April, according to U.N. International Maritime Organization figures. The fresh attacks threatened to choke off the flow of traffic in the strait just as countries hoped to restore normal shipping practices and ease the global economic strain of the war.
One tanker was traveling off the coast of Oman when it was hit and caught fire, the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations center said. Iranian state television said the liquefied natural gas tanker came under attack after ignoring warnings but did not directly claim the assault.
The other two ships sustained some damage, but no one was injured, and both continued on their way, the U.K. maritime agency said.
Tehran, which has repeatedly declared that only its approved route through the strait is safe, is suspected of attacking other ships that have used another route close to the Omani shore.
Location details provided by the U.K. agency show all three attacks occurred off the coast of Oman or the neighboring United Arab Emirates, making it likely that the ships were using the route near Oman.
U.S. Central Command did not immediately respond to a request for comment about Tuesday’s attacks.
The U.S. is eager to press ahead with negotiations with Iran aimed at fully reopening the strait, rolling back Tehran’s disputed nuclear program and reaching a permanent end to the war launched Feb. 28. Aninterim dealhasbeen strained.
Previous attacks in the strait have sparked retaliatory strikes by the U.S. Iran thenattacked Gulf Arab states.
In peacetime, a fifth of all traded oil and natural gas passed through the channel.
Meanwhile, talks between Iran and the U.S. appeared to be on hold until after the burial ofIran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed at the beginning of the war. Mourners at his funeral have called forthe death of U.S. President Donald Trump.
Authorities flew Khamenei’s body to the Shiite seminary city of Qom, where mourners honored him Tuesday.
One tanker was carrying liquid natural gas south through the strait near Limah, Oman, when a projectile hit the left-side engine room and sparked a fire, the U.K. Maritime Trade Operations center said.
Iranian state TV, quoting anonymous sources, implied that Tehran carried out the assault on a tanker it said was carrying natural gas from Qatar. However, there was no official claim from the Islamic Republic for the attack.
Majed Al-Ansari, a spokesperson for the Qatari Foreign Ministry, said the Qatari tanker Al Rekayyat was targeted in an “unacceptable attack” on international navigation and global energy security. He called it a “serious and explicit violation” of international law.
In a post on X, he said Qatar holds Iran “fully legally responsible” for the attack.
Later Tuesday, the U.K. maritime agency reported that an oil tanker was hit on its left side as it exited the strait near the Omani-Emirati border. A third tanker was struck by a drone off Oman, the agency said.
Iran’s joint military command warned last Thursday that all oil tankers moving throughthe straitmust use its approved routes. It also said that interference by U.S. forces in the strait “will be met with a rapid and decisive reaction.”
But the Joint Maritime Information Center, a multinational body overseen by the U.S. Navy, told shippers Monday that the route around Oman “has been expanded and remains available for all traffic.”
Ships going to the north on the Iranian route must register with Tehran. Those going south work with Oman and the U.S.
Speaking Monday at the White House, Trump warned Iran that it would need to “make a deal, or we’re going to finish the job.”
“I’d rather make a deal, because I don’t want to affect 91 million people,” Trump said. “We can knock down their bridges in one hour. We can knock out their energy supply.”
Iran and the United States agreedas part of an interim dealto allow ships to pass without paying charges for 60 days. But Tehran insisted it must control the vessels’ routes and later charge fees for passage, which would upend decades of practice in the waterway.
The U.S. and many Gulf Arab statessay they will not agree to Iran charging for passage through the strait. An effort by Oman and the U.N. to launch a new route near Oman’s shore earlier sparked attacks across the Mideast.
The data firm Kpler reported that over last weekend at least 108 ships crossed through the strait using various routes.
Iranian state television aired live images Tuesday of hundreds of thousands of people walking toward Jamkaran Mosque, just south of Qom, for a funeral service for Khamenei. Shiites believe the mosque once hosted Muhammad al-Mahdi, the 12th and last Shiite imam, who disappeared in the 9th century and is supposed to one day reappear to bring justice to the world.
Images of Khamenei and his son,Iran’s new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, were displayed on banners and posters held by mourners. Mojtaba Khamenei has yet to make an appearance at the funeral ceremonies, which are unfolding over several days. He is believed to be in hiding after reportedly being wounded in the airstrike that killed his father.
Authorities have shut down streets, airspace and daily life for the mourning, which began Saturday.
The government-run IRNA news agency reported that Khamenei’s body was taken Tuesday night to Najaf, Iraq. Processions are planned for Wednesday in Najaf and Karbala, the two holy cities of Iraqi Shiism. Iraq has a sizable Shiite population and is home to major Shiite religious sites and centers of learning.
Khamenei, who was 86, will then be returned to Iran to be buried Thursday at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad, his birthplace.

item.title: 法国总统马克龙访问叙利亚首都时发生爆炸
item.description:
叙利亚大马士革（美联社）——叙利亚内政部称，法国总统在对叙利亚进行具有里程碑意义的访问时会见了法国总统，周二，叙利亚首都发生爆炸，造成至少 18 人受伤。
这是大马士革一周内发生的第二起袭击事件，对艾哈迈德·萨拉总统来说是一次挫折，因为他欢迎自 2024 年底长期独裁者巴沙尔·阿萨德比叛乱组织被推翻以来第一位到访的西方主要领导人。叙利亚新统治者在维护控制权的同时一直在努力应对暴力爆发，但首都基本上是和平的。
爆炸发生时，法国总统马克龙正在总统府。爱丽舍宫的一名官员表示，他很安全，与沙拉的会面仍在继续，并要求匿名讨论马克龙的安全问题。
没有任何组织立即声称对此负责。
美联社记者查尔斯·德莱德斯马报道，法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙访问叙利亚时，大马士革发生爆炸；当地消息称，有四人受伤。
“没有什么能够抑制叙利亚男女生活在一个完全主权、安全、多元化和统一的叙利亚的愿望，”马克龙在 X 小时后说道。“今天早上，我见到了叙利亚的多样性。我看到了尊严、勇气和决心。”
随后，沙拉和马克龙宣布同意在十多年后重新任命大使，标志着两国外交关系的重大恢复。
“我们的会议标志着一个历史里程碑，”沙拉说。法国于 2012 年关闭了大使馆，但在 2025 年初象征性地重新开放。
马克龙在推动欧洲和美国放弃对阿萨德领导下的叙利亚实施的大部分制裁方面发挥了重要作用。他在前往大马士革之前，于周二晚些时候前往土耳其安卡拉参加北约峰会，沙拉也将出席。
叙利亚媒体报道称，马克龙下榻的四季酒店附近的爆炸现场冒出大量浓烟。社交媒体上的视频显示，旅游部总部和大马士革国家博物馆附近一条繁忙的街道上，一辆面包车和一辆摩托车着火，并有血迹。
叙利亚官方媒体报道，内政部在一份声明中表示，一枚炸弹被放置在垃圾桶中，另一枚放置在一辆停放的汽车中。据称，其中四名伤者是警察，目前还没有人员死亡的报道。
周四，一枚爆炸装置在司法宫附近的一家咖啡馆引爆，造成至少 10 人死亡、20 多人受伤。
叙利亚政府认为，马克龙的访问以及与巴黎和法国大公司签署的十多项协议是该国新当局重建该国的重大推动力，该国在阿萨德领导下经历了长达14年的起义和内战。
其中一项协议是启动归还属于阿萨德已故叔父里法特·阿萨德的约 5100 万欧元（5830 万美元）非法资产的程序。其他协议包括重建霍姆斯市被毁的水电基础设施、在叙利亚中央银行进行金融改革时提供技术援助以及加强大马士革机场的货运基础设施。
叙利亚外交部官员告诉美联社：“这次访问的结果证实，叙利亚正在稳步迈向基于共同利益和相互尊重的国际伙伴关系的新阶段。”他表示，袭击的肇事者将被绳之以法。“破坏国家稳定的企图不会改变这一轨迹。”该官员根据规定要求匿名。
这些爆炸对沙拉来说是一个挑战，沙拉一直致力于全面控制叙利亚，吸引对他的伊斯兰领导的统治持怀疑态度的少数群体，并赢得西方政府的支持，这些政府对他过去领导与基地组织有关联的沙姆解放组织感到担忧。他的政府承诺在几十年的独裁统治后进行政治和经济改革。
叙利亚冲突造成近五十万人死亡，数百万人流离失所。基础设施已成废墟。尽管其他国家和企业做出了大量投资承诺，但该国仍需要数千亿美元来重建并使数百万人摆脱贫困。
在抵达总统府之前，马克龙会见了叙利亚民间社会成员，但他的办公室没有透露细节。
切哈耶布从贝鲁特报道。美联社巴黎撰稿人约翰·莱斯特和西尔维·科贝特以及布鲁塞尔撰稿人萨姆·麦克尼尔也做出了贡献。

item.title: 特朗普再次要求格陵兰岛，北约公布数十亿美元的武器交易以证明其火力
item.description:
ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — PresidentDonald Trumpon Tuesday insisted that the United States should be incontrol of Greenlandrather than NATO ally Denmark, renewing tensions in Europe even as the trans-Atlantic military alliance was announcing billions in arms deals at a summit in anattempt to appeasethe mercurial U.S. leader.
Trump called the semiautonomous island “an important part” for the United States, as herepeated the false claimthat it’s surrounded by Chinese and Russian ships and said he won’t let Greenland be threatened.
“That should be controlled by the United States, not by Denmark,” Trump told reporters during a meeting with Turkish PresidentRecep Tayyip Erdoganin Ankara.
President Donald Trump, left, meets with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan as he arrives for the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, Tuesday, July 7, 2026. (Osmancan Gürdoğan, Pool Photo via AP)
The NATO alliance was founded on the principle that its 32 members will defend each others’ territory and not threaten to seize it. At the summit, European countries and the alliance’s secretary-general,Mark Rutte, were already working overtime to address another longstanding Trump complaint: that European allies do not spend enough on their own defense.
Separately, Trump announced that the U.S. will lift sanctions on Turkey that were issued after Ankara purchased a Russian missile defense system that led to the country being kicked out of the F-35 fighter jet program — in a nod tohis warm ties with summit host Erdogan.
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, center, participates in a media statement with European Council President Antonio Costa, left, and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen during the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, Tuesday, July 7, 2026. (AP Photo/Hussein Malla)
Turkey’s purchase in 2019 of Russian-madeS-400missile defense systems sparked years of tensions, despite the warm personal relationship between Trump and Erdogan dating back to the U.S. president’s first term.
Legal hurdles remain before Turkey could be fully admitted back to the U.S. F-35 program, but the removal of sanctions issued under the Countering America’s Adversaries Through Sanctions Act would help ease that process. Regaining access to the F-35s is a top goal of Erdogan.
“We’re going to be taking the sanctions off, OK?” Trump said in response to a question, saying Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth were working on the issue.
Front row from left, U.S. Ambassador to Turkey Tom Barrack, U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and U.S. Ambassador to NATO Matthew G. Whitaker attend the formal welcome for President Donald Trump at the NATO summit at the Bestepe Presidential Palace in Ankara, Turkey, Tuesday, July 7, 2026. (AP Photo/Alex Brandon)
Trump said the possibility of selling F-35s to Turkey is “something certainly we’d consider” given the countries’ relationship, and that “Turkey’s been, in many ways, much more loyal than other countries that we think would be loyal.”
Erdogan expressed hope that the U.S. will sell the F-35s, saying the U.S. president always stands by his word.
Trump and Erdogan showed off their fondness for each other. Erdogan greeted Trump with an elaborate ceremony involving military officials on horseback and jets overhead emitting red, white and blue smoke.
Asked what makes their relationship so strong, Trump said there’s “a chemistry that works between us,” adding that “Sometimes you get along with the toughest people, like him.”
Turkey’s access to U.S. F-35s could complicate relationships elsewhere.Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahusaid he has urged Trump not to sell the fighter jets to Turkey, saying it would put Israel in danger.
“This is not a force for peace and stability,” Netanyahu said on CNN. “When you give them that power, you’re going to see aggression its wake.”
President Donald Trump shakes hands with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan upon arriving for the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, Tuesday, July 7, 2026. (AP Photo/Alex Brandon)
There is also opposition among U.S. lawmakers to Turkey having the F-35s as long as the Russian missile defense system remains in its possession. Even if sanctions are lifted, the Trump administration still faces restrictions under U.S. law that prevents Turkey from being able to purchase the fighter jets if it owns the S-400s.
Earlier in the day, NATO showcased military projects worth billions of dollars — an investment Rutte called “money well spent” and one clearly meant to try to satisfy Trump.
Rutte was speaking to government ministers and defense industry officials at a forum billed as NATO’s “big reveal,” to the thrum of techno music.
NATO does not own weapons — these are the property of member countries — but it has 14 AWACS early warning radar surveillance planes that are about 50 years old, along with newer surveillance drones.
A deal to replace the aging planes was announced Tuesday. Swedish manufacturer Saab will supply up to 10 new GlobalEye surveillance aircraft for a 10-nation consortium, Swedish Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson announced.
Some projects will be paid for with funds from asystem of cheap loansfor defense purposes set up by the European Union, comprising up to $170 billion raised on capital markets.
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, listens, as Sweden’s Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson speaks at the opening of the NATO Defense Industry Forum on the sidelines of the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, Tuesday, July 7, 2026. (AP Photo/Hussein Malla)
Representatives from 15 nations announced a multinational effort to buy air-to-air refueling and transport planes from Airbus. Then Rutte announced a four-country effort to purchase as many as five new Triton surveillance drones.
Rutte had told reporters on the eve of the two-daysummitthat “we will announce tens of billions in new contracts.” However, at Tuesday’s event, no dollar figures were given and the display included some projects long since agreed.
Separately, Ukrainian PresidentVolodymyr Zelenskyymade a fresh appeal for his country to be allowed to join the alliance, saying his country’s armed forces are highly experienced and would boost NATO’s defense capabilities.
He highlighted Ukraine’s ability to strike deep inside Russia and hit oil refineries and other energy targets. He said Ukraine’s armed forces are “eliminating” on average 30,000 Russian troops every month. He is set tomeet with Trump on Wednesdayin Ankara.
Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, left, arrives for the NATO Summit in Ankara, Turkey, Tuesday, July 7, 2026. (Metin Aktaş, Pool Photo via AP)
“Frankly we take no pride in this,” Zelenskyy said, noting that the war with Russia — now in its fifth year — is one “we did not seek but one we are forced to fight.”
Concern is mounting among some European countries that Russia might be preparing a hybrid attack — a combination of conventional warfare with tactics like cyberattacks — on the continent asRussian President Vladimir Putinstrugglesto secure victory in Ukraine.
Yet a senior NATO official, speaking on the summit’s sidelines, said that despite some “reckless” actions by Russia, including airspace violations over Poland, Romania and Estonia, the alliance has been successful in deterring Moscow from any potential attack on a member country. The official insisted on anonymity to brief reporters.
Associated Press writers Jill Lawless in London, Andy Wilks in Istanbul and Michelle L. Price and Collin Binkley in Washington contributed to this report.

item.title: ICE 通过电子邮件派警员前往一名男子的家中。现在他正在起诉
item.description:
纽约州北部的一名居民起诉美国移民和海关执法局，称其派联邦官员到他家，并通过他发给该机构前任负责人的电子邮件发出警告。
美国公民大卫·斯特里弗 (David Streever) 的律师称，今年 6 月，两名警官出现在他位于罗切斯特的家中，并向他的妻子出示了一份警告通知，告知他几个月前发送的电子邮件被视为威胁。斯特里弗于 1 月份向时任 ICE 代理主任托德·莱昂斯 (Todd Lyons) 发送了这封电子邮件，此前一名移民官员在一次反 ICE 示威活动中射杀了明尼阿波利斯居民蕾妮·古德 (Renee Good)。
在电子邮件中，斯特里弗称里昂是“一个可怕的人”，“永远不知道和平”。他表示，该机构周一在华盛顿特区提起诉讼，侵犯了他的第一修正案权利。
斯特里弗是纽约州北部至少两名因在网上批评 ICE 后收到联邦警告的居民之一。总部位于费城的个人权利与表达基金会代表斯特里弗，并表示提起诉讼是因为斯特里弗的言论自由权受到侵犯。
“这显然受到第一修正案的保护，”该基金会的律师亚当·斯坦博 (Adam Steinbaugh) 说。“这是在政治演讲的背景下。”
ICE 的代表此前拒绝就向斯特里弗发出的警告发表评论，理由是调查正在进行，该机构周一也没有立即发表评论。该诉讼还点名了国土安全部部长马克韦恩·穆林，他的办公室发表了一份声明，称“任何关于国土安全部及其下属机构试图‘压制’言论自由的指控都是绝对错误的。”
声明称：“任何袭击或威胁我们执法人员的人都将面临后果。”
这封三段电子邮件的全部内容都以“下一步是什么”为主题，并提到了纳粹德国的一名领导人：“你是一个可怕的人，将作为美国的屠夫莱因哈德·海德里希载入史册。
“即使我们看到这些视频，你保护明尼苏达州明显执行的方式也会导致你的垮台。就连特朗普也会在最后一刻对你发起攻击，而你将成为一个悲伤的、被鄙视的人，为自己可悲的弱点感到羞愧，活活吃掉自己。
“你永远不会平静。你会寻求迷失自己，逃避了解自己真相的负担。但无论你走到哪里，你都会找到自己。你会折磨自己，直到你在地球上的最后一天。”
斯坦博说，当斯特里弗从芬兰回来时，联邦特工还试图在纽约市的一家酒店与他对峙，但被酒店工作人员拒绝了。
就在纽约初选期间，联邦官员前往斯特里弗家的同一周，官员们在纽约初选期间的一个投票地点拜访了民意调查工作人员佩吉琳·贡耶亚 (Paigelynne Gonyea)，就社交媒体帖子与她对质。
戈尼亚认为，这一警告源于在一篇贴有枪杀古德的 ICE 官员乔纳森·罗斯 (Jonathan Ross) 的照片的帖子中写道，“我认为今天是乔纳森被起诉的好日子”。她在一月份发布了这篇文章，当时罗斯已经被新闻媒体确认了。
美国国土安全部发言人劳伦·比斯 (Lauren Bis) 分享了一张来自 Gonyea 的不同社交媒体帖子的图片，她说 Gonyea 分享了罗斯的地址。该帖子的部分内容已被编辑。
比斯在 6 月的一份声明中表示，Gonyea“通过在网上发布 ICE 执法人员的地址而犯下了联邦罪行”，并且“如果你对我们的官员进行人肉搜索，我们将调查你，并将你绳之以法。”
纽约总检察长办公室的一名代表表示，该办公室了解这两名居民与联邦特工的联系。该代表表示，该办公室一直在审查戈尼亚和联邦特工在投票期间的互动。

item.title: 爱达荷州一位母亲称她的双胞胎孩子在接种疫苗后死亡，她被指控谋杀
item.description:
当局称，爱达荷州一名妇女称，她的双胞胎双胞胎去年在接种疫苗后死亡，她面临与死亡有关的谋杀指控。
根据法庭记录和帕耶特警察局的一份声明，大陪审团于 6 月 29 日对安德里亚·肖 (Andrea Shaw) 提出两项一级谋杀罪，她被控于 2025 年 5 月窒息其 18 个月大的双胞胎。
去年，肖在由卫生与公共服务部部长小罗伯特·F·肯尼迪创立的反疫苗组织“儿童健康防御”制作的网络节目中表示，她的双胞胎在接种疫苗后死亡。肯尼迪自 2024 年 12 月正式辞去主席职务加入唐纳德·特朗普总统政府以来，就不再与该组织有任何关系。
23 岁的肖于周二被博伊西警察逮捕，并于周四被提审。她被交纳了 200 万美元的保释金，如果罪名成立或承认一级谋杀罪，她可能面临终身监禁或死刑。她的下一次出庭时间是 7 月 14 日。
肖的代表律师乔·菲利塞蒂在短信中写道，她“否认一切”，并且国家“无法证明”刑事指控。
“我们将全心全意地捍卫她，”菲利塞蒂补充道。
帕耶特警察局和帕耶特县检察官办公室周一拒绝发表评论。
2025 年 5 月，肖在儿童健康保卫节目中露面时说，她发现她的双胞胎在接种流感和其他疾病疫苗几天后死在自己的房间里。
“两名护士同时给他们注射了疫苗，”肖说。“然后他们就生病了。”
医学专家指出，有争议的儿童疫苗——甲型肝炎疫苗、流感疫苗和百白破疫苗——对儿童来说是安全有效的，并得到了各个医疗团体的推荐。
肖还是儿童健康保护组织和其他组织对美国儿科学会提起的联邦诉讼的原告。该诉讼于一月份向华盛顿联邦法院提起，指控美国儿科学会“在一家企业中发挥核心作用，几十年来在儿童疫苗接种计划的安全性问题上欺骗美国家庭”，从而敲诈勒索。在诉讼中，肖被描述为一位母亲，“她的孩子在按照美国儿科学会指南进行常规疫苗接种后死亡”。
美国儿科学会已要求法院驳回诉讼，并在 4 月份的一份法庭文件中声称，这是“针对”该学会及其“在疫苗政策中使用科学支持的证据”的“运动中的最新信件”。
一月份，当美国卫生官员对儿童疫苗指南进行了广泛修改，放弃了几项通用建议时，儿科医生和其他专家感到震惊。多年来帮助领导反疫苗运动的肯尼迪表示，这些变化更好地使美国与其他国家保持一致，“同时加强透明度和知情同意”。
今年三月，一名联邦法官阻止了这些改变，并表示肯尼迪可能违反了修改关键疫苗咨询委员会的联邦程序。但法官的命令并不是最终决定；这些封锁是暂时的，等待审判或简易判决的决定。
凯勒蒂从凤凰城报道。美联社路易斯维尔撰稿人劳拉·昂加尔 (Laura Ungar) 对此作出了贡献。

item.title: 曼哈顿高层建筑柱子弯曲仍不稳定 迫使疏散
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——官员们表示，曼哈顿一栋在建的高层建筑周二仍然不稳定，柱子弯曲和地板下垂引发了人们对倒塌的担忧，迫使大楼和附近其他建筑撤离。
这座1970年代的办公楼正在改建为豪华公寓，是制药巨头辉瑞公司的前全球总部。上午 8 点左右，初步报告称有砖块掉落，消防队员赶往中央火车站和地标性克莱斯勒大厦附近繁忙的走廊。
纽约市官员正在使用无人机监视该建筑，以避免不得不将人员送入其中。市长佐兰·马姆达尼赛德 (Zohran Mamdanisaid) 称，附近一所学校有 400 名学生，位于疏散建筑物之列。
“这座建筑仍然不稳定，”马姆达尼在现场的新闻发布会上说。“这是一个极其严重的情况。”
消防队长约翰·埃斯波西托 (John Esposito) 表示，从这座钢结构建筑的建造方式来看，“它不会完全倒塌，而更多的是局部倒塌。”
2026 年 7 月 7 日星期二，纽约，在该地区的建筑物被疏散后，一辆消防车停在第三大道和东 42 街的交叉口。（美联社照片/安吉丽娜·卡萨尼斯）
该市建筑专员艾哈迈德·蒂加尼表示，官员们没有看到碎片从建筑物上掉落的证据。尽管如此，附近的街道仍然对人和车辆关闭。马姆达尼表示，目前还没有人员受伤的报告。
领导该项目的建筑公司 Gensler 表示，办公楼改建为住宅将增加十几层楼，并重新设计毗邻的一座塔楼。Gensler 表示，该项目共有 1,600 多个单元，被誉为该市历史上规模最大的改建项目。
Gensler 的发言人没有回复寻求置评的语音邮件和电子邮件。
官员发现21层和22层的两根柱子弯曲，21层和26层的地板下垂。
马姆达尼说，急救人员和市政府官员正在与项目工程师密切合作，制定加固受影响地板的计划。如果认为安全，工程师将进入并开始维修。
“这是每分钟的评估，”市长说。
建筑专员表示，工人们需要添加应急梁和柱以稳定受损的梁和柱。
市长周二上午表示：“我们现在的首要任务是确保该地区居民和该地区工作人员的安全。”
此报道已更新，以更正市政府官员将建筑物的高度修改为 37 层，而不是 38 层。
伊萨吉雷从纽约林登赫斯特报道。

item.title: 在缅因州候选人面临性侵犯指控后，民主党开始撤回普拉特纳的支持
item.description:
A woman who previously dated Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner said he drunkenly forced her to have sex after she told him to stop, according to aPolitico report, leading prominent supporters to pull their endorsements and throwing a must-win race for the party into turmoil.
Platner denied the allegation on Monday, but said he would be considering next steps for his campaign.
“Regardless of the inaccuracy of the reporting but mindful of the political reality it will inflict, we’re taking the time to reflect on the best path forward,” he said in a video released on social media.
Jenny Racicot, who lives in Maine, told Politico that Platner entered her home in 2021 while drunk and assaulted her. Racicot said she had been in an on-and-off relationship with Platner, but she cut off contact with him after that night and told him the incident wasn’t consensual. A voicemail left at a number listed for Racicot seeking comment did not receive an immediate response, but she said in a CNN interview on Monday evening that she opted not to fight back for fear of Platner, a former Marine, becoming more violent.
AP correspondent Haya Panjwani reports on reactions to a new allegation against a Senate Democratic nominee.
“He violated multiple layers of consent that night,” Racicot said.
Platner’s campaign did not immediately respond to an email and phone message from The Associated Press seeking comment.
“Any accusation of non-consensual behavior is categorically false,” Platner said in his video.
Democratic U.S. Senate candidate Graham Platner speaks during a primary election night watch party after winning the Democratic nomination June 9, 2026, in Blue Hill, Maine. (AP Photo/Robert F. Bukaty, File)
Platner won the Democratic nomination last month, setting himself up to face Republican Sen. Susan Collins, who has beaten back previous attempts to dislodge her from the seat that she’s held for nearly three decades.
Although Platner has long been controversial, the sexual allegation sparked a flight away from the candidate, who canceled a handful of town hall events. The main campaign arm of Senate Democrats called on Platner to drop out and said it would spend no money on the race, which is considered critical to control of the chamber, if he is the nominee.
“Graham Platner needs to immediately withdraw as the Democratic nominee for Senate and allow Maine Democrats the opportunity to choose a new candidate who can defeat Susan Collins,” Kirsten Gillibrand, chair of the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee, and Chuck Schumer, the top Senate Democrat, said in a joint statement.
The Democratic National Committee sent out an email soliciting money for Senate races hours after the Politico report posted, but Maine was not one of them. Ken Martin, the party chair, said, “Maine Democrats should select a new nominee.”
Rep. Ro Khanna, a California Democrat who stood by Platner even as the candidate faced previous controversies, said Monday’s allegation was enough. “I’ve been very clear that sexual assault or violence against women is a red line,” Khanna said. “These allegations are very serious and credible. Graham Platner should drop out from the race. I am withdrawing my endorsement.”
Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., addresses delegates at the South Carolina Democratic Party convention, May 30, 2026, in Columbia, S.C. (AP Photo/Meg Kinnard, File)
Arizona Sen. Ruben Gallego and Massachusetts Sen. Elizabeth Warren announced they were pulling their endorsements and called on Platner to drop out. The Democratic leaders of Maine’s legislature and top officials at the state Democratic Party did the same.
“This Senate race comes at a pivotal moment in the struggle against a government, supported by Senator Collins, that serves the interests of the wealthy and powerful at the expense of ordinary Maine people. It is essential that we refocus this campaign on that struggle,” party chair Charlie Dingman, vice chair Imke Schessler and executive director Devon Murphy-Anderson said in a joint statement.
“These allegations are appalling,” she said. “Nevertheless, it is not up to me to choose the Democratic nominee for Senate.”
State lawallows Platner to be replaced on the ballot if he withdraws by July 13. The replacement candidate must be named by July 27.
The Associated Press generally does not name victims of sexual assault, but in this case Racicot spoke in an interview with Politico.
Graham Platner, Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate, speaks at a campaign event June 5, 2026, in Bar Harbor, Maine. (AP Photo/Robert F. Bukaty, File)
Platner had never before held elected office, and Democratic leaders in Washington preferred Gov. Janet Mills in the primary. However, Mills, 78, dropped out as Platner, 41, consolidated support with help from progressive leaders at a time when Democratic voters have grown disenchanted with the party establishment.
While some Democratscame around to support himafter his commanding primary win, Platner’s controversial history had already left others openly despairing of their chances of winning the race. A veteran who also worked for a private security contractor, Platner has a chest tattoorecognized as a Nazi symbol, reportedlysexted with other womenshortly after getting married and had a history of inflammatory comments on social media.
In 2013, Platner posted on Reddit that people shouldn’t get so drunk “they wind up having sex with someone they don’t mean to” and that sexual assault victims should “just take some responsibility for themselves.” He’s since apologized for the post and says he no longer holds those beliefs.
The New York Times also reported that Platner had volatile relationships with previous girlfriends, one of whom saidan argument became physical.Platner denied the allegation.
Hasan Piker, a leftist commentator and streamer who backed Platner, seemed to reverse himself Monday following the Politico report.
“If new evidence presents itself, I’m going to change my perspective — it’s that simple,” Piker said during a livestream on Twitch, adding: “This is a clear-cut instance of verifiable sexual assault allegations. It’s completely irredeemable.”
Our Revolution, a progressive organization founded by Sen. Bernie Sanders of Vermont, said Platner should withdraw because the allegations “are too serious to treat as a distraction from the campaign or the issues.”
It also hinted at the potential battle over who would replace Platner.
“Whoever leads this movement forward must be someone who has actually lived the fight Graham Platner ran on: a record with working people, with unions, against corporate money, already tested and trusted by the same base that delivered this result,” said a statement from Joseph Geevarghese, executive director of Our Revolution.
Platner had pitched himself as a blue-collar oysterman and veteran who could reach disaffected voters. But as controversies mounted, some state Democrats had heartburn, embodied by Mills’ refusal to endorse Platner after she dropped out of the primary. Chatter circulated about possible replacements, including former state senator and logger Troy Jackson and Secretary of State Shenna Bellows.
“I’ve known this has been coming,” said Marie Follayttar, a Democrat and community organizer in Maine, talking about the growing whispers inside the state’s small population that had been bracing for yet another revelation surrounding Platner. “I’ve been scared and I’ve been sick waiting.”
Mike Connelly, a business owner and Democrat in Brunswick, Maine, said in an interview that he wants Platner to drop out after the latest allegations. But Connelly said he’d vote for him if he stays in.
“I would vote for a comatose Democrat before I would vote for Susan Collins,” Connelly said.
Jesse Bedayn contributed from Austin, Texas, Meg Kinnard contributed from Columbia, S.C., and Ali Swenson contributed from New York.

item.title: 参议员麦康奈尔正在“继续康复”，但在长期住院后细节很少
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——肯塔基州参议员米奇·麦康奈尔的工作人员上周表示，参议员在参议院休会期间正在医院“继续康复”。但他的办公室没有透露这位前共和党领导人在为期数周的住院期间的病情细节，也没有透露他下周参议院返回时是否会在国会大厦的细节。
根据麦康奈尔办公室的一份声明，麦康奈尔于 6 月 14 日入院，仅表示他“正在接受优质的护理”。一周后的一份声明称，他那周不会投票。周四，一份新的声明称，他“继续进步”，并“感谢他在医院继续康复期间所获得的大量支持。”
此后，他的办公室没有发布任何最新消息，发言人周一也没有回复置评请求。
这位参议员近年来多次住院治疗，随后出现了未具体说明的健康问题，而参议院共和党人在中期选举前的最后几个月已经以微弱优势获得多数席位。麦康奈尔现年 84 岁，是历史上任职时间最长的参议院领袖，随后卸任。他的最后一个任期将于一月结束。
当麦康奈尔仍担任共和党领袖时，他于 2023 年 3 月因脑震荡入院，并在华盛顿一家酒店摔倒后错过了数周的工作。那年夏天，他回国后两次在新闻发布会上僵住了，在同事和工作人员前来帮助他之前，他茫然地凝视着前方。一年后，他在走出共和党午餐会时摔倒并扭伤了手腕。
麦康奈尔在童年时期就患有小儿麻痹症，他很早就承认成年后走路和爬楼梯有些困难。2019年，他还在肯塔基州的家中绊倒，并接受了肩部骨折的手术。
麦康奈尔于 1984 年首次当选参议院议员，并从 2007 年到去年担任共和党领袖，在此期间担任多数党和少数党领袖。作为一名普通参议员，他一直很活跃，在众议院开会时出席工作，经常使用轮椅出行。

item.title: 最高法院不会阻止德克萨斯州执行要求下载应用程序进行年龄验证的法律
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——美国最高法院周一拒绝阻止德克萨斯州执行一项州法律，该法律要求应用程序商店验证用户的年龄，并为寻求在手机上下载应用程序或进行应用内购买的未成年人征得家长同意。
法官塞缪尔·阿利托 (Samuel Alito) 在两项一句话命令中驳回了原告的请愿书，原告声称《德克萨斯州应用商店责任法案》侵犯了用户的宪法权利和言论自由权。
上个月，美国第五巡回上诉法院的三名法官组成的小组裁定该法律可以生效。去年 12 月，该小组暂停了地方法院关于该法律违宪的裁决。
起诉阻止该法律的原告包括计算机和通信行业协会和学生参与推进德克萨斯州。德克萨斯州总检察长肯·帕克斯顿是这两起案件的被告。
原告律师辩称，该法律试图限制对受第一修正案保护的内容（包括新闻和教育材料）的访问，这是不允许的。
“公平和公共利益支持救济，因为保护第一修正案的权利——以及父母按照自己认为合适的方式监督孩子的权利，而不是政府告诉他们应该监督的权利——始终符合公共利益，”学生参与推进德克萨斯州的律师写道。
帕克斯顿办公室的律师辩称，该法律保护儿童免受“危险的现代产品”的侵害。
他们写道：“有权访问应用程序商店和移动设备（例如平板电脑或智能手机）的儿童可能会下载任意数量的软件应用程序，可能会同意侵犯儿童的隐私和出售儿童的数据，并在未经父母同意甚至父母不知情的情况下接触任何可以想象的内容。”

item.title: 梅里诺最后时刻的进球让西班牙队进入四分之一决赛并结束了罗纳尔多的世界杯生涯
item.description:
得克萨斯州阿灵顿（美联社）——在常规时间的最后几分钟，米克尔·梅里诺仍然坐在替补席上，西班牙队主教练路易斯·德拉富恩特担心，由于加时赛迫在眉睫，他是否能尽可能长时间地留在替补席上。
这位阿森纳前锋让所有这些烦恼都变得毫无意义。
下半场补时第一分钟梅里诺进球，西班牙队周一以1-0击败葡萄牙队，结束了巨星克里斯蒂亚诺·罗纳尔多的世界杯生涯。
梅里诺刚刚被撞倒，裁判吹罚了犯规。当葡萄牙队的贝尔纳多·席尔瓦争论时，梅里诺将球回传，跑向球门，在费兰·托雷斯中场漂亮的传球后轻松击败门将迪奥戈·科斯塔。
德拉富恩特通过翻译说道：“他是世界上同位置上最好的球员之一，他为我们带来了出色的成绩和精彩的进球。”“我想表达比赛后期替补登场的重要性。不仅是今天，其他比赛中的贡献也是巨大的。”
西班牙队连续35场不败，自2010年赢得唯一的世界杯冠军以来首次晋级四分之一决赛。西班牙队将于周五在加利福尼亚州英格尔伍德迎战比利时队，后者以4-1战胜美国队。
罗纳尔多试图帮助葡萄牙队连续第二次进入四分之一决赛。相反，这位国际进球数（146 粒）和出场次数（233 粒）历史领先者的职业生涯已经结束。
梅里诺直到第85分钟才上场，他的单挑表现展现了一定的多才多艺，帮助阿森纳在今年春天赢得了20多年来的首个英超冠军。
对于在世界杯期间年满 30 岁的梅里诺来说，右脚手术缩短了他在阿森纳的赛季，他能否参加世界杯还存疑。
在西班牙队一年中最重要的时刻，他以全新的双腿完成了比赛，他的左脚射入了球网的左角。与队友拥抱后，梅里诺在角落里绕着旗帜转了一圈，然后握紧了拳头，发出一声喉间尖叫。
这就是他的父亲米格尔·梅里诺三十多年前在西班牙俱乐部生涯期间的庆祝方式。两年前，他的儿子在最后时刻进球击败东道主德国，将西班牙送入欧洲杯半决赛后，首次效仿。
这是米克尔·梅里诺在世界杯上的首个进球，也是国家队的第11个进球。
“还有什么更好的庆祝方式呢？”梅里诺说。“你记得所有的好和坏，今年对我来说有困难的时刻。”
伊比利亚半岛的对手们105年前在马德里首次进行友谊赛，最近一次的交锋与他们最近一次的世界杯比赛形成了鲜明的对比。
八年前，罗纳尔多在小组赛揭幕战3-3战平西班牙的比赛中上演了他在世界杯上的唯一帽子戏法，这场比赛被认为是世界杯上最好的比赛之一。
这位41岁的超级巨星在本届世界杯上打进3球，但在对阵西班牙球员乌奈·西蒙的比赛中并没有太多机会，后者将自己的世界杯不失球纪录扩大到了609分钟。西班牙队成为第一支在世界杯上连续六场不胜的球队。
最好的机会是罗纳尔多在第37分钟的漂亮后踢，当时若昂·菲利克斯的头球从西蒙的左肩上偏转，飞向罗纳尔多。他用右脚轻弹皮球，但皮球足够柔软，让西蒙有时间恢复并跳跃性抢球。
“明天我会像今天醒来一样，问心无愧，”罗纳尔多说道。一天前，他重申了自己的第六届世界杯将是他的最后一次世界杯。“我尽了最大努力。我随葡萄牙队赢得了三个冠军。在克里斯蒂亚诺之前，葡萄牙还没有赢得过任何冠军。所以，我很高兴。我随国家队赢得的最大的冠军是2016年（欧洲杯），老实说，这对我来说与世界杯具有同等的意义。”
补时最后八分钟，葡萄牙拼命扳平比分。席尔瓦的头球攻门稍稍高出球网。
自从葡萄牙在一场引人入胜的欧洲国家联赛决赛中2-2战平并进入点球大战以来，距离葡萄牙队击败西班牙队仅剩两天，这场防守斗争就开始了。
另一场世界杯比赛是西班牙以 1-0 获胜——同样是在 16 强赛中——当时西班牙队在南非赢得了冠军。
“这是一场精彩的比赛。两支出色的球队，”德拉富恩特说道。“正如我们所说，这就像一场预料之中的决赛。正如预料的那样，我们必须忍受到最后。”
直到西班牙不再受苦——多亏了它的超级替补。

item.title: 国际足联解除美国球星弗拉林·巴洛贡的禁赛后需要了解什么
item.description:
FIFA’sstunning decisionto lift the suspension of a star U.S. player riled the host country’s World Cup opponent, Belgium, and sent soccer fans — and political leaders — into a frenzy over the influence President Donald Trump may have had over the extremely rare ruling.
Hours before kickoff Monday, FIFA dismissedBelgium’s challengeto the most-debated political intervention in aWorld Cupin decades. U.S. forward Folarin Balogun had faced a mandatory ban from the match after receiving a red card last week, but FIFA lifted his suspension on Sunday followinga call Trump madeto the global soccer organization’spresident, Gianni Infantino.
Despite Balogun’s start in Seattle, the U.S. lost 4-1 to Belgium.
Born in New York to Nigerian parents, raised in London, and playing in the French league, Balogun’sbirthright citizenshipmade him eligible for the U.S.
The Belgian soccer federation wants an explanation from FIFA about a decision to let U.S forward Folarin Balogun play at the World Cup despite getting a red card in his previous game. Belgium takes on the United States later Monday for a spot in the quarterfinals.
Securing his commitment to play on the American team was a coup and it had paid off; the 25-year-old led the team’s World Cup scoring with three goals.
All was well until Wednesday when hestepped onopponent Tarik Muharemović’s ankle in a2-0 winover Bosnia-Herzegovina in the round of 32.
The decision to send off Balogunwas disputed— his movement seemed clumsy but not malicious. But once a referee decides to issue a red card, the punishment is usually straightforward: The player is excluded from the rest of the game and — until now, at least — a suspension for the next game is automatic.
If the ban had stayed in place, replacing Balogun in the lineup posed a big challenge for coach Mauricio Pochettino.
The U.S. had plenty of attacking players in wider or deeper roles, but few with the combination of physical power and goal-scoring ability for the center-forward role that the rest of the offense focuses around. Likely replacement Ricardo Pepi hadn’t scored in four World Cup games.
There is typically no appeals process against the automatic one-game ban, only for longer sanctions usually applied to the most serious fouls like violent conduct or racism.
In its decision to let Balogun play against Belgium, FIFA cited article 27 of its disciplinary code, which says a “judicial body” can “fully or partially suspend the implementation of a disciplinary measure.” Balogun could yet get that one-game suspension on top of any future punishment if he commits a similar offense again in the next year.
In a statement Monday, FIFA’s disciplinary committee gave more details on its decision. It said it found Balogun guilty of two breaches of its disciplinary code: one related to the red card and another for celebrating with his teammates on the field after the Bosnia match despite having been ejected. It imposed a fine of $40,000 but suspended the automatic one-match ban for a probationary period of one year.
“The sanction remains dormant during the probationary period and will only be activated if he commits another infringement of a similar nature and gravity during that one-year period,” the committee said.
Infantino insisted in a social media post that FIFA’s disciplinary committee acted with independence and judged cases such as Balogun’s on “applicable regulations and the specific facts.” Article 27 doesn’t lay out any requirements for which cases are eligible under the rarely used rule.
Last year, FIFA suspended two games of a three-game ban for one of soccer’s biggest-ever stars, Cristiano Ronaldo. That left himfree to play in the opening games of the World Cupfor Portugal. He did serve the remaining one game ban in a qualifier.
Balogun’s case seems to be the first since 1962 in which a sending-off during a World Cup match didn’t result in a suspension. On that occasion, the president of host nation Chile argued for Brazilian midfielder Garrincha to be allowed to play the final after he had kicked a Chilean opponent.
“Thank you to FIFA for doing what was right, and reversing a great injustice!” Trump said Sunday on social media after Balogun’s suspension was lifted. On Monday, Trump defended his outreach to Infantino, saying he merely pointed out that the referee’s call against Balogun seemed like a bad one and warranted a closer look.
Infantino and Trump have developed awell-known relationship. The Swiss soccer official became a regular visitor to the Oval Office as the U.S. prepared to host the World Cup. He gave Trump aFIFA peace prizeat the World Cup draw in December, an award the organization hasn’t presented to anyone else before or since.
FIFA’s statutes prohibit governments from intervening in the independence of soccer bodies managing their own affairs. FIFA regularly suspends member federations where governments have interfered in decision-making.
Pochettino, the U.S. coach, applauded FIFA’s move Sunday and said the initial on-field ruling against Balogun was “completely unfair.”
The Belgian soccer federation said it was “astonished” when the news of FIFA’s intervention broke. Coach Rudi Garcia likened the decision to April Fools’ Day.
On Monday afternoon, a FIFA appeals judge dismissed Belgium’s legal challenge fewer than eight hours before kickoff. The Belgian soccer body “is not a party to the proceedings and, as such, has no standing to appeal the decision,”FIFA said in a statement.
FIFA’s disciplinary code says suspensions of two games or less typically can’t be appealed — though that would generally apply to teams wanting a suspension lifted, not reimposed.
As Europe woke to the news Monday, the Instagram account of Belgian Prime Minister Bart de Wever’s cat, Maximus — a social media celebrity in his own right — weighed in with apicturecaptioned: “Red card? I’m still going to play!”
European soccer body UEFAcriticized FIFAfor an “incomprehensible and unjustifiable decision” and warned “the integrity of the game is at stake.”
In an apparent response to UEFA’s criticism, FIFA’s disciplinary committee said “reviewing the legal consequences of red cards in football is nothing new in the modern game.”
Norway coach Ståle Solbakken weighed in after his team stunned Brazil on Sunday to reach the quarterfinals.
“What about the next red card? What happens then?” he said. “Is there going to be some committee somewhere that is going to take that card away?”
Former England great Wayne Rooney said on the BBC: “Infantino, he should be ashamed of this because I think the sportsmanship of this game is in question here.”
Ex-Sweden striker Zlatan Ibrahimović was a prominent voice welcoming the decision.
“First of all, he should not get a red card and then they should have come quicker, this call,” Ibrahimovic said on Fox Sports. “I’m happy for the U.S. team because the U.S. team has been amazing but Balo has been super-amazing.”
England coach Thomas Tuchel predicted this could set off a flood of complaints and appeals over other on-field decisions affecting key players at the World Cup.
“Where to draw the line is the question that I ask,” he said after England beat Mexico 3-2 for a quarterfinal spot.
“Our yellow card from the first minute against Declan Rice, we can now debate endlessly. I think it’s not a yellow card. Do we get this back?”
AP Sports Writer Graham Dunbar in Geneva contributed to this report.

item.title: 基利安·姆巴佩谴责巴拉圭参议员在世界杯比赛后的种族主义言论
item.description:
法国球星基利安·姆巴佩翁周一谴责巴拉圭参议员在巴拉圭在世界杯 16 强中输给法国队后发表的种族主义言论。
姆巴佩称巴拉圭自由激进党参议员塞莱斯特·阿马里拉是“卑鄙的女人”，“不配”在巴拉圭国会任职。
姆巴佩在 X 上写道：“由于你们的鲁莽和无耻的种族主义，整个世界已经忘记了你们的球员在本届世界杯上所经历的旅程和所取得的历史性努力。”
周六，姆巴佩在法国战胜巴拉圭的比赛中主罚制胜点球后，阿马里拉在 X 上发表了一系列种族主义言论，嘲笑这位法国队长的出身、成长、教育和外表。法国队晋级四分之一决赛，周四将对阵摩洛哥。
周一晚些时候，阿马里拉在社交媒体上用法语和西班牙语向姆巴佩发表了一封公开信，她在信中表示，她的问题在于球员，而不是法国。她写道，她很遗憾以混血儿时受到的“同样的侮辱”来虐待姆巴佩，并删除了自己的帖子。
但她也要求姆巴佩道歉，指责他在对她的评论中存在性别暴力，并威胁如果他不撤回这些评论，就会采取法律行动。
美联社向法国队媒体官员发送电子邮件，请其对阿马里拉的信发表评论。
巴拉圭政府周一下午发表声明，谴责阿马里拉的言论“违背了我国倡导的和平共处和尊重人类尊严的价值观和原则”。声明补充说，参议员的言论既不代表巴拉圭政府，也不代表巴拉圭人民。
法国足协周一谴责阿马里拉的言论“完全令人憎恶”且“不可接受”，并补充说将把此事提交给检察官。
法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙和体育部长玛丽娜·法拉利表达了对国家队队长的支持。
法拉利在 X 上写道：“通过针对基利安·姆巴佩，这位参议员正在攻击我们队长所体现的一切以及我们国家所代表的一切：自由、平等和博爱。”
“基利安·姆巴佩又进了一个球。这一次是反对种族主义，”马克龙在 X 上写道，并补充说队长得到了他的“全力支持”。
法国队助理教练盖伊·斯特凡周一也谴责了这一言论。
“用三个词来说，这是愤怒、卑鄙、可耻，”他说。
在周六的比赛之前，前巴拉圭门将何塞·路易斯·奇拉维特将法国称为“一支来自非洲的球队”。FFF主席菲利普·迪亚洛表示，奇拉维特“曾经是一位伟大的门将”，但现在“陷入了耻辱”。

item.title: 泰勒·斯威夫特 (Taylor Swift) 的婚纱设计师让艺术而非名人主导迪奥 (Dior)
item.description:
巴黎（美联社）——乔纳森·安德森 (Jonathan Anderson) 抵达巴黎时装周，时尚界仍在等待他为泰勒·斯威夫特 (Taylor Swift) 制作的迪奥 (Dior) 婚纱。
周一，第一天，他试图给它一些别的东西来看待。
斯威夫特在纽约麦迪逊广场花园与美国国家橄榄球联盟 (NFL) 明星特拉维斯·凯尔斯 (Travis Kelce) 结婚三天后，两人均身着迪奥 (Dior) 礼服，安德森 (Anderson) 带着受美国艺术家琳达·本格里斯 (Lynda Benglis) 启发的雕塑般的重褶高级时装系列重返秀台。
对于 LVMH 旗下品牌和 41 岁的北爱尔兰设计师安德森来说，这一委托是一个重大举措。安德森一年前被任命负责彻底改革迪奥的所有时装系列。
几个月来，行业观察家一直把赌注押在美国名字上，比如拉尔夫·劳伦(Ralph Lauren)，或者斯威夫特经常穿的薇薇安·韦斯特伍德(Vivienne Westwood)。
世界希望看到的一件衣服是安德森不会展示的一件。
所以周一他改变了话题——艺术。
该系列试图将话题从斯威夫特的隐藏礼服转移到 Benglis 的作品上，Benglis 自 20 世纪 60 年代末以来以将乳胶倒在画廊地板上，让金属折叠和下垂成形而闻名。
迪奥工作室被视为她工作室的一个版本——平面织物被压制、打结和弯曲成三个维度的地方。
Benglis 将扁平材料弯曲成型；最终，高级时装也是如此。
衣服也遵循了这个想法。银箔花瓣裙随着每一步而移动。
无肩带银色金属丝长袍上系着一个超大蝴蝶结。裤子和衬衫均采用手工压紧的褶皱制成。
Dior 标志性的 Bar 夹克是该品牌自 1947 年以来一直沿用的高腰款式，经过多种改造：采用带有磨边流苏的蕨绿色花呢，采用折叠成巨大蝴蝶结的灰色千鸟格，还有一次在下摆处采用宽松的雪纺线。
其他造型完全由刺绣丝花打造而成。
一件蓝色薄纱的大扇子铺在一件连衣裙的前面。
手袋采用金属褶皱设计，其中四个是 Benglis 亲自设计的。
法国又迎来了另一场热浪，气温超过 30 摄氏度（86 华氏度）。
迪奥向粉丝们发出了邀请函，嘉宾们在罗丹博物馆花园的时装秀中使用了邀请函。
前排混杂着流行歌星和艺术家。
歌手萨布丽娜·卡彭特和演员乔什·奥康纳坐在嘉宾中，其中包括佩丽冉卡·乔普拉、尼克·乔纳斯、娜奥米·沃茨、丽贝卡·弗格森和艾里珊·钟。
尽管令人兴奋，安德森的赌注很简单：世界上最著名的时装屋可以承受奇怪的感觉。
人们经常将他与竞争对手 Chanel 的新设计师 Matthieu Blazy 相比较，后者本月为歌手 Dua Lipa 设计了婚纱。
这个季节现在有一个独特的特点：它的两个最大的故事是不允许任何人看到的连衣裙。
正如时装传统所规定的那样，这场秀以新娘结束。
安德森推出了一件浅色无肩带长裙，上面覆盖着手工褶皱雪纺长面纱，饰有蒲公英羽毛和仙人掌花刺绣。
这是安德森本周展示的第二件婚纱，也是唯一一件任何人都可以拍摄的婚纱。

item.title: 关于残疾人权利活动家的《成为休曼》将为多伦多电影节开幕
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——导演西安·海德 (Siân Heder) 执导的关于已故残疾人权利活动家朱迪思·休曼 (Judith Heumann) 的电影《成为休曼》(Being Heumann) 将为第 51 届多伦多国际电影节开幕。
电影节组织者周二宣布，由露丝·马德利 (Ruth Madeley) 饰演休曼 (Heumann) 主演的《成为休曼》(Being Heumann) 将于 9 月 10 日加拿大电影节开幕之夜进行全球首映。电影节将持续到 9 月 20 日。
休曼于 2023 年去世，因其长期倡导和游说最终制定联邦《美国残疾人法案》而被称为“残疾人权利运动之母”。休曼在 2 岁时失去了行走能力，也是 2020 年奥斯卡提名电影《跛子营》中的核心人物。
《成为休曼》是海德继 2021 年电影《CODA》之后的续作，该片荣获奥斯卡最佳影片奖。这次胜利标志着聋人社区的一个里程碑，也标志着苹果公司首次赢得好莱坞最高奖项。苹果还发布了“Being Heumann”。
TIFF 首席执行官卡梅伦·贝利 (Cameron Bailey) 在一份声明中表示：“我们很高兴能够以 Siân Heder 继奥斯卡获奖作品《CODA》之后鼓舞人心的后续作品拉开今年的电影节序幕。”“《Being Heumann》以露丝·马德利 (Ruth Madeley) 的精彩表演为主角，讲述朱迪·休曼 (Judy Heumann) 的故事，朱迪·休曼是一位改变世界的无障碍倡导者。”
该电影节是秋季电影首映的启动平台之一，还宣布了苏珊娜·怀特 (Susanna White) 的辛西娅·埃里沃 (Cynthia Erivo) 主演的法律惊悚片《Prima Facie》和许镇浩 (Hur Jin-ho) 的韩国惊悚片《刺客》(The Assassin(s)) 的全球首映。
该故事已更新，以纠正电影《Crip Camp》拍摄于 2020 年，而不是 2000 年。

item.title: 特雷西·埃利斯·罗斯 (Tracee Ellis Ross) 即将在百老汇首次亮相，并完成了她的愿望清单
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——特蕾西·埃利斯·罗西斯（Tracee Ellis Rossis）开始注意到给她带来快乐的小事：比如卷发棒发出的令人满意的咔哒声，或者橄榄的味道。这是她现在的工作。
这位演员兼制片人将于周二开始在百老汇首演《Every Brilliant Thing》，这是今年夏天更令人振奋和快乐的戏剧之一，尽管它深入探讨了抑郁症的主题。
这是一个叙述者的故事，他列出了一系列事情，向他或她抑郁的母亲证明生活是值得的——比如小鸭子、意大利肉酱面和在公共场合跳舞。第 999,996 号是：“将一张完整的壁纸剥离。”
“令人难以置信的是，当你开始这样做时，你确实开始注意到以前从未想过的事情，”罗斯说。“它们无处不在，这就是这件作品如此美丽的原因：它改变了你看待世界的方式。”
罗斯进入百老汇角色，该角色由丹尼尔·雷德克里夫原创，后来由玛莉丝卡·哈吉塔担任。这是一场个人表演，但观众会帮忙，一些人被要求朗读纸片上的内容，另一些人则假装开车、制作袜子木偶或扮​​演叙述者的父母。
罗斯将帮助选择并说服观众加入她的节目，当顾客首先入场时，她会在过道里闲逛聊天。她会寻求善意，甚至一些抵制。
“这是一种为材料服务的感觉，而一点点不情愿实际上是我们需要扮演的角色的一种特殊品质，”她说。
由邓肯·麦克米伦（Duncan Macmillan）和乔尼·多纳霍（Jonny Donahoe）撰写的《每件精彩的事情》允许叙述者即兴发挥，并改变一些精彩的事情以适应他们的背景。因此，拉德克利夫的“穿斗篷”在罗斯的例子中可以变成“芭蕾舞短裙”。
“这份清单必须让他们感到真实，”麦克米伦说。“他们在人生不同阶段会发现的辉煌之处，因人而异。”
该剧根据新演员的年龄和背景（最喜欢的麦片或卡通片）进行了重写，并根据他们在青少年时期可能迷恋的人以及他们一路上可能发现的精彩电影进行了调整。
“他们与物质产生联系有自己的个人原因，但他们也是令人难以置信的有趣、开放、慷慨、谦逊的人，我们支持他们，”麦克米伦说。
该剧只有 70 分钟，但罗斯从不休息，做群众工作，在短短三周的排练期间，她基本上被要求记住 40 页的独白。
“感觉就像我正在吞下一条鲸鱼，并学习如何通过我的手指、通过我的身体、通过我的声音和通过我的心消化它，”她说。
罗斯毕业于布朗大学，并在威廉·埃斯珀工作室学习表演，她的职业生涯始于戏剧、电影和电视的试镜。她因在《黑人》和《女朋友》等节目中扮演的角色而闻名。
百老汇一直在她的愿望清单上，但她表示，主演一部以充满希望的方式与其他人建立联系的戏剧正是“我的最佳选择”。
“这是一个关于我们很多人都在挣扎的真实故事，但它是通过生命线的视角来讲述的，让生活变得有价值——这就是我的愿望清单上的一切，”她说。
“有时候，面对感情和事实，希望可能会让人感到畏惧，但我确实认为，拥有能力和地图来实现其他事情可以平衡这些时刻，从而为你提供下一个机会。”
她抵制住了看雷德克利夫或哈吉泰扮演这个角色的诱惑：“一旦我脑子里浮现了别人的表演或想法，这对我来说就很难了。”
当被问到一件更个人的精彩事情时，五个孩子之一的罗斯很快给出了答案：“那一刻，你和你的兄弟姐妹互相看了一眼，因为你确切地知道发生了什么，只有我们五个人知道，但没有其他人知道。”
